description: ol-undefined-var (issue #113): a procedure's own parameter lives only in its own lexical frame (spec/execution-model.md:317-327). Reading `:secret` inside `f`'s body (line 2) is fine — it is `f`'s own parameter — but reading `:secret` again at the top level after `f` returns (line 5) is a read with no visible declaration in the top-level scope chain, so it raises `ol-undefined-var` at that read's own span. This reproduces and locks in the fix for a genuine scoping bug in the rule's first draft, which used a flat whole-program declaration model that let a procedure's parameters leak to every other scope.
